info.title: Tapestry API Reference
info.description: Documentation for all routes in the API

contacts.description: Array of phone contacts with optional display names

deviceCountry.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code (e.g., US, BR, PT)

limit.description: Number of items per page (max 200, default 10)

phoneHash.description: Hashed phone number of the contact to invite

platform.description: Platform through which the invite will be sent

inviteLink.description: Complete referral link with invite code

inviteCode.description: The invite code from the referral link

inviterProfileId.description: Profile ID of the person who invited

friendsOnly.description: Filter to only show pets that are friends (have CONNECTION relationships) with the current user's pet

startAt.description: Unix timestamp in milliseconds when announcement should become active

endAt.description: Unix timestamp in milliseconds when announcement should expire

startAt.description: Unix timestamp in milliseconds when announcement should become active

endAt.description: Unix timestamp in milliseconds when announcement should expire

startAtAfter.description: Unix timestamp in milliseconds - filter announcements that start after this time

startAtBefore.description: Unix timestamp in milliseconds - filter announcements that start before this time

endAtAfter.description: Unix timestamp in milliseconds - filter announcements that end after this time

endAtBefore.description: Unix timestamp in milliseconds - filter announcements that end before this time

id.description: Optional unique identifier for the profile

referredById.description: Optional ID of the referring profile

campaignId.description: Optional campaign ID to track referrals for specific campaigns

inviteCode.description: Optional invite code to link this signup to a specific contact invite

post.description: For creating a user profile. The endpoint will first check to see if the wallet exists elsewhere on the graph. If it does, we will create a new profile that is namespaced to your app and associate with the wallet you pass in. If the wallet does not yet exist, we will create a node for the wallet, a node for the profile (namespaced to your app) and an edge indicating that the wallet you passed in is associated with the profile on your app.

get.description: Retrieve all referral connections for a user profile, including both users who made referrals (upstream) and users who were referred (downstream) by this profile, with depth indicating the number of connection levels (defaults to 2, maximum 6). For example, depth=1 shows direct referrals, while depth=2 includes referrals made by those direct referrals.

schema.description: Optional filter to specify the depth of upstream referral connections (profiles that referred this user). Defaults to 2 if no value is specified.

parameters.description: Optional filter to specify the depth of upstream referral connections (profiles that referred this user). Defaults to 2 if no value is specified.
parameters.name: upstream

schema.description: Optional filter to specify the depth of downstream referral connections (profiles referred by this user). Defaults to 2 if no value is specified.

parameters.description: Optional filter to specify the depth of downstream referral connections (profiles referred by this user). Defaults to 2 if no value is specified.
parameters.name: downstream

post.summary: 🎯 Extract ZK compression data for frontend transaction building
post.description: Returns all ZK compression data needed for frontend to build follow/unfollow transactions with fresh blockhash. Supports both follow and unfollow operations based on type parameter.

remainingAccounts.description: Remaining account public keys as base58 strings

400.description: The request payload or query string parameter you passed was not valid

get.description: Filter and sort data using optional query parameters: 'requireFields' for a comma-separated list of properties that must not be null in results, 'orderByField' with 'orderByDirection' (asc/desc) to sort the results, and 'filters' for advanced multi-field filtering

schema.description: Filter format: 'field:operator:value' or 'field:value' for exact match. Operators: =, !=, >, <, >=, <=, CONTAINS. Examples: 'age:>=:18,title:CONTAINS:hello,status:published' or 'description:' for null checks.

parameters.description: Filter format: 'field:operator:value' or 'field:value' for exact match. Operators: =, !=, >, <, >=, <=, CONTAINS. Examples: 'age:>=:18,title:CONTAINS:hello,status:published' or 'description:' for null checks.
parameters.name: filters

400.description: The request payload or query string parameter you passed was not valid

get.description: Get aggregated data for contents using filters. Use 'requireFields' for a comma-separated list of properties that must not be null in results, 'filters' for advanced multi-field filtering, and 'aggregation' for the aggregation operation.

schema.description: Filter format: 'field:operator:value' or 'field:value' for exact match. Operators: =, !=, >, <, >=, <=, CONTAINS. Examples: 'age:>=:18,title:CONTAINS:hello,status:published' or 'description:' for null checks.

parameters.description: Filter format: 'field:operator:value' or 'field:value' for exact match. Operators: =, !=, >, <, >=, <=, CONTAINS. Examples: 'age:>=:18,title:CONTAINS:hello,status:published' or 'description:' for null checks.
parameters.name: filters

schema.description: Aggregation format: 'operation:field'. Operations: SUM, AVG, MIN, MAX. Example: 'SUM:price' for sum of price field.

parameters.description: Aggregation format: 'operation:field'. Operations: SUM, AVG, MIN, MAX. Example: 'SUM:price' for sum of price field.
parameters.name: aggregation

get.summary: Get activity feed for a specific user profile.
